videos.title: NEW Pagani Codalunga Speedster is the ULTIMATE Hypercar!
videos.summary_source_text:
NEW Pagani Codalunga Speedster is the ULTIMATE Hypercar! Mat is reviewing the all-new, ultra-rare Pagani Codalunga Speedster. He’ll walk you around the design, interior, engine & drivetrain, and find out how to fit the roof. 

Explore new and used car deals with Carwow: https://bit.ly/Change-Your-Car-25-06

This is one of the rarest and most expensive cars Mat has ever reviewed! 

He’s flown over to Italy to visit Pagani, and he’s been allowed to get up close and personal with the new Pagani Codalunga Speedster! 

It’s ultra-rare, with only 10 to ever be sold! The question is, how does it differ from the Codalunga Coupe? And is it worth the €11,000,000 price tag?! Let’s take a closer look… 

Although the shape is still largely similar to the coupe, there are a few unique differences. Up top, there’s now a blade through the large air intake in the bumper, along with larger vents in the bonnet to help cool the radiator. Along the side, the gull-wing doors have been replaced with regular doors, which therefore means the wings in front and behind the doors are all-new. The huge air intake scoops behind the cabin are also more prominent. Around the back, there are loads of active aero components and ceramic-coated exhaust tips. What's more, unlike the Coupe edition, the speedster comes with six (although two are hidden lower down). Also, a special mention to this green paint - it looks absolutely magnificent! 

The interior of the Codlinga Speedster is the definition of refinement. The only materials you’ll find in here are metal, leather, wood, fabric or carbon - there isn’t one single piece of plastic! What’s more, the fabric on the doors and seats is so rare and expensive that it costs more than a regular family car! All buttons and switches just feel expensive when you touch them, and there’s a surprising amount of storage available. There is also bespoke luggage available, which, once again, is coated in the expensive fabric. There’s also one rare button which we haven’t seen on a car before - a fire extinguisher switch! 

The Codalunga Speedster uses the same engine as found in the Pagani Utopia. That means it’s powered by a 6-litre twin-turbo V12 that delivers 864hp and 1,10Nm. All this power is sent to the rear wheels via a 7-speed manual gearbox. 

So the question is - if you could, would you pay the eye-watering, pre-tax price of €11,000,000?! Stick with Mat to see if you should! Mat is reviewing the all-new, ultra-rare Pagani Codalunga Speedster. He’ll walk you around the design, interior, engine & drivetrain, and find out how to fit the roof.

videos.title: TRADE STOVE IS HOT! Carolina Hurricanes in Trade Rumors for Alexander Nikishin
videos.summary_source_text:
TRADE STOVE IS HOT! Carolina Hurricanes in Trade Rumors for Alexander Nikishin After a wild Tuesday of trades, it seems like the NHL is not slowing down as more trades went down on Wednesday. Despite the Washington Capitals getting Alex Tuch, the Carolina Hurricanes are still in a great spot. That said, they have cropped up in trade rumors regarding Alexander Nikishin. Does it mean anything? On the Thursday Edition of Locked On Hurricanes, Zach begins the show by sharing his thoughts on the Nikishin trade rumor situation. Darren Dreger posted on X/Twitter that the Hurricanes are hearing interest from teams about the young Russian defenseman, who just won the Stanley Cup. Taking interest doesn't mean that they will trade him away. Does it make sense to move on from him? Secondly, the Hurricanes announced on Wednesday night that they extended Jusso Valimaki to a one-year, $900,000 deal to keep him in the organization after acquiring him last season from the Utah Mammoth. He played a big role for the Chicago Wolves last season in the AHL, en route to a Calder Cup Final appearance. What does it mean moving forward? Lastly, Zach concludes the show by sharing his thoughts on what could be an interesting offseason for the Hurricanes. With most of the forward core signed, outside of two guys on the defense, what could the Hurricanes do to prepare for next season? All of that and more, on the latest edition of Locked On Hurricanes.
